{"step_by_step_thinking": "The documents clearly state that the β-oxidation of odd-chain fatty acids produces one molecule of acetyl-CoA and one molecule of propionyl-CoA. The final step in the process converts propionyl-CoA into succinyl-CoA, which is a bridge to the TCA cycle. Therefore, the primary product of β-oxidation of odd-chain fatty acids is propionyl-CoA.", "answer_choice": "B"}
